entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

entities.name: albumin binding-mediated half-life extension

attributes.structural_basis: C18 fatty diacid acylation via linker at lysine 26

entities.context: Semaglutide is a modified analog with alpha-aminoisobutyric acid (Aib) at position 8 and arginine at position 34, acylated with a C18 fatty diacid via a linker at lysine 26, which extends the half-life by promoting albumin binding.

entities.name: US10888605B2
entities.context: Patent ID: US10888605B2; Filing Date: January 28, 2020; Assignee: Novo Nordisk AS

relations.evidence: Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

Semaglutide is a modified analog with alpha-aminoisobutyric acid (Aib) at position 8 and arginine at position 34, acylated with a C18 fatty diacid via a linker at lysine 26, which extends the half-life by promoting albumin binding.

Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

Semaglutide is a modified analog with alpha-aminoisobutyric acid (Aib) at position 8 and arginine at position 34, acylated with a C18 fatty diacid via a linker at lysine 26, which extends the half-life by promoting albumin binding. This enables once-weekly subcutaneous administration.
Patent ID: US10888605B2; Assignee: Novo Nordisk AS
The invention covers pharmaceutical formulations containing the GLP-1 peptide semaglutide with phenol content not exceeding 0.01% (w/w).
